title: Embedded payments and subscriptions using Accounts v2
description: Build an integration with embedded payments and subscriptions on Accounts v2.

steps.title: Create and onboard a connected account
steps.description: Enable sellers (connected accounts) on your platform to accept payments from their customers.

nodes.title: Create an account
nodes.description: Create a connected account as a <LinkMerchant>merchant</LinkMerchant> so it can accept payments from its customers, and also configure it as a <LinkCustomer>customer</LinkCustomer> so you can charge that account subscription fees.
nodes.review_prompt: Confirm the implementation calls the intended Stripe API, reuses values needed by later steps, and handles API errors without exposing secrets.

nodes.title: Simulate connected account onboarding
nodes.description: Run this test helper to before your connected accounts can start accepting payments on your platform, they need to onboard by inputting KYC information. To expedite the process, use this test helper to bypass the connected account onboarding. It will input <Link>test KYC values</Link> that enable payments capability on the connected account.
nodes.review_prompt: Run the helper flow and confirm it advances test state without adding helper-only parameters to application code.

nodes.title: Create an account link
nodes.description: In your actual integration, create an account link to collect KYC information from your connected account, which will render a <LinkHosted>Stripe-hosted onboarding flow</LinkHosted>. Alternatively, you can use <LinkEmbedded>embedded onboarding</LinkEmbedded> to integrate the onboarding component into your application or use <LinkApi>API onboarding</LinkApi> to create a customized onboarding UI using Stripe's APIs.
nodes.review_prompt: Confirm the implementation calls the intended Stripe API, reuses values needed by later steps, and handles API errors without exposing secrets.

nodes.title: Complete KYC data collection
nodes.description: Your connected account can use the generated account link to complete KYC data collection. This node isn't required for this example but will be necessary in your actual implementation.
nodes.review_prompt: Open the app and confirm the user-facing flow works as described.

nodes.title: Wait for the account to onboard
nodes.description: Listen for the webhook event to identify when Stripe has completed KYC verification. In your actual integration, you will need to create an event destination to listen for the <Link>event</Link>.
nodes.review_prompt: Run the relevant Stripe CLI trigger or complete the upstream flow, then confirm the handler receives and verifies the expected event.

steps.title: Accept embedded payments
steps.description: Create a <Link>charge</Link> directly on the connected account and immediately transfer a fee to your platform.

nodes.title: Create a checkout interface
nodes.description: Create a Checkout Session for the connected account to accept payments. The connected account's customer will be directed to a <LinkHosted>Stripe-hosted prebuilt payment form</LinkHosted> optimized for conversion. Alternatively, you can also <LinkEmbedded>embed a prebuilt payment form</LinkEmbedded> on your site or use the API to build a more <LinkApi>customized checkout experience</LinkApi>.
nodes.review_prompt: Confirm the implementation calls the intended Stripe API, reuses values needed by later steps, and handles API errors without exposing secrets.

nodes.title: Accept a payment
nodes.description: The connected account's customer can then complete a payment on the checkout surface. Click on the link below and fill in the <Link>test card information</Link> with the card number as 4000 0000 0000 0077 to run a successful payment. Use a valid future date, such as 12/31 and any three-digit CVC.
nodes.review_prompt: Open the app and confirm the user-facing flow works as described.

nodes.title: Verify that the payment is complete
nodes.description: Listen for the <Link>webhook event</Link> to identify if the payment successfully went through.
nodes.review_prompt: Run the relevant Stripe CLI trigger or complete the upstream flow, then confirm the handler receives and verifies the expected event.

steps.title: Charge subscriptions
steps.description: Charge your connected accounts subscription fees.

nodes.title: Set up a subscription plan
nodes.description: Create a <Link>product</Link> to configure the amount and interval for subscriptions.
nodes.review_prompt: Confirm the implementation calls the intended Stripe API, reuses values needed by later steps, and handles API errors without exposing secrets.

nodes.title: Attach a default payment method for the account
nodes.description: Create a <Link>SetupIntent</Link> to attach a payment method for the account. We'll use the account's balance to pay for subscription fees.
nodes.review_prompt: Confirm the implementation calls the intended Stripe API, reuses values needed by later steps, and handles API errors without exposing secrets.

nodes.title: Add funds to the connected account
nodes.description: Run this test helper to to pay the subscription using the account's balance, connected accounts <Link>must have funds</Link> in their Stripe balance. For now, use this test helper to add funds.
nodes.review_prompt: Run the helper flow and confirm it advances test state without adding helper-only parameters to application code.

nodes.title: Charge the subscription
nodes.description: Charge the subscription and take the fees directly from the account's balance.
nodes.review_prompt: Confirm the implementation calls the intended Stripe API, reuses values needed by later steps, and handles API errors without exposing secrets.

nodes.title: Verify that the subscription succeeded
nodes.description: Listen for the <Link>webhook event</Link> to identify if the invoice successfully went through.
nodes.review_prompt: Run the relevant Stripe CLI trigger or complete the upstream flow, then confirm the handler receives and verifies the expected event.
